title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) - Easy Authentic Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with this easy recipe. A comforting, healthy, and flavorful Middle Eastern classic perfect for any meal.

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of warm Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a healthy and flavorful Middle Eastern staple.

description: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is a beloved Middle Eastern comfort food, renowned for its hearty, wholesome appeal. This vibrant, nutritious soup is packed with aromatic spices, tender red lentils, and fresh vegetables, creating a deeply satisfying and flavorful experience. Perfect for a chilly evening or a light, healthy meal, Adas offers a taste of authentic Lebanese home cooking.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain well and set aside. Chop the onion, mince the garlic, and dice the carrot. Measure out all your spices to have them ready.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the diced carrot and cook for 3-5 minutes, allowing them to slightly soften. Add the ground cumin, turmeric, and coriander, stirring constantly for 1 minute until fragrant. This step helps to bloom the spices and enhance their flavor.

instructions.title: Combine Lentils and Liquid
instructions.text: Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine them with the vegetables and spices. Pour in the 6 cups of water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend (Optional) and Season
instructions.text: Once the lentils are cooked, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup to your desired consistency. For a creamier soup, blend more; for a chunkier soup, blend less or mash with a spoon. Alternatively, carefully transfer a portion of the soup to a regular blender, blend, and return to the pot. Stir in the salt and black pepper.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the soup from the heat and stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding more salt, pepper, or lemon juice to your preference. Ladle the hot soup into bowls, drizzle with a little extra virgin olive oil, and garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley. Serve immediately with warm pita bread or a side salad.

tips: Always rinse your lentils thoroughly before cooking to remove any debris and improve digestibility. This is a common practice in Middle Eastern kitchens.
For extra depth of flavor, toast the dry spices lightly in a separate dry pan for 30 seconds before adding them to the pot. Be careful not to burn them.
Adas soup thickens as it cools. If reheating, you may need to add a splash of water or broth to reach your desired consistency. It stores well in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
article:
Lebanese cuisine is a vibrant tapestry of fresh ingredients, bold flavors, and comforting dishes. Among its most beloved and ubiquitous offerings is Adas, the humble yet incredibly satisfying Lebanese Red Lentil Soup. More than just a meal, Adas is a warm embrace in a bowl, a testament to the power of simple ingredients transformed into something extraordinary. Whether you're seeking nourishment, comfort, or a taste of the Middle East, this creamy, zesty soup is a culinary journey you won't want to miss. It's a cherished staple, loved across generations for its ease, affordability, and incredible depth of flavor.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?
Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as "Adas," is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish, especially in Lebanon. This simple yet profoundly flavorful soup features red lentils, fresh lemon juice, and aromatic vegetables and spices. Unlike some heartier stews, Adas is typically smooth and creamy, often pureed to achieve its silky texture and bright golden-orange hue. It embodies Lebanese cooking: fresh, wholesome, and bursting with natural flavors. Adas stands out for its unique tangy zest from fresh lemon, perfectly balancing earthy lentils and rich olive oil. Served as a main course with bread or a comforting appetizer, Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a cherished culinary heritage, offering both sustenance and soul-soothing warmth.

## Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup
The beauty of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup lies in its simplicity and the quality of its core ingredients. You won't find a long list of obscure items
